title: Exciting expo to wow Garden Route
description: GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - Garden Route Mall and...
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - Garden Route Mall and George Tourism have developed an expo event that is set to become the Garden Route's most prominent annual showcase for players in the field of events, tourism and activities (ETA).

The first ETA Expo - appropriately named as a wordplay on the tourism-related abbreviation for 'estimated time of arrival' - will be held at the mall from 27 February to 2 March.

Stalls have to be booked by 21 February.


> Stall space is limited, and allocations will be made on a first-come, first-served basis.


Businesses offering destinations, products and services related to events, tourism or activities anywhere along the Garden Route, are encouraged to book their spot.

The expo allows you to showcase your offerings to a targeted audience that actively seeks fresh and exciting options for their upcoming events, as well as to network and build valuable relationships with fellow exhibitors and industry professionals.

Sonja Anderson, sales and events coordinator, urges professionals who are serious about marketing their offerings in these fields to reserve a space.

"This is an incredible opportunity to network and showcase your product or service - not only to thousands of potential customers but also to your industry colleagues, who could be prospects for collaboration," she says.


> To book, send an e-mail to Anderson at [email protected], or call her on 044 887 0044 before 21 February.


Canoeing is also a very popular pastime for locals and visitors in the Garden Route. Enjoying a scenic paddle along the Touw River in Wilderness should be on everyone's bucket list. Photo: Jeff Ayliffe‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
